query: COP30 utilities 150 billion annual energy transition investment pledge participating companies

mako.raw:
{"query":"COP30 utilities 150 billion annual energy transition investment pledge participating companies","model":"mako","response_time_ms":993,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/climate-energy/cop-30-big-pledges-renewables-industry-ambition-falters-ending-fossil-fuels--ecmii-2025-11-27/","title":"COP30: Big pledges on renewables and industry, but ambition falters on ending fossil fuels | Reuters","content":"- Utilities pledge $148 billion annually for grid expansion and energy storage"},{"url":"https://www.downtoearth.org.in/climate-change/cop30-global-utilities-raise-annual-energy-transition-investment-to-148-billion-with-grids-taking-centre-stage-in-belem","title":"COP30 Energy Transition: Utilities Boost Investment to $148B, Focus on Grids","content":"COP30: Global utilities raise annual energy-transition investment to $148 billion, with grids taking centre stage in Belem
The updated investment plan means UNEZA members, comprising many of the world’s largest utility companies, will collectively mobilise over $1 trillion in transition investments by 2030, since the group’s formation at COP28.
“Utilities worldwide are mobilising with investments that are tangible, substantial and aligned with the need for a more resilient and sustainable energy system,” said Jasim Husain Thabet, Group CEO of TAQA and UNEZA co-chair.
November 14’s ministerial meeting marked a rare moment of alignment between utilities, governments and MDBs.
According to the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), $670 billion per year is required globally for grid expansion and strengthening between now and 2030—nearly double today’s investment levels."},{"url":"https://www.energyconnects.com/news/utilities/2025/november/global-utilities-set-out-1-trillion-investment-plans-at-cop30/","title":"Global utilities set out $1 trillion investment plans at COP30","content":"Global utilities have announced upgraded annual investment plans that will see their energy transition spend rise to $148 billion per year, up from previously stated ambitions of $117 billion.
Members will invest $66 billion per year in renewables, and $82 billion in grids and storage.
Jasim Husain Thabet, Group Chief Executive Officer of TAQA, founder and co-chair of UNEZA, said: “UNEZA was established at COP28 as a platform for cooperation and decisive energy transition action from the global utilities community.
The alliance is co-chaired by founding member TAQA headquartered in Abu Dhabi, and the UK’s SSE, and operates under the guidance of the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) and the Climate High-Level Champions."},{"url":"https://news.un.org/en/story/2025/11/1166433","title":"Belém COP30 delivers climate finance boost and a pledge to plan fossil fuel transition | UN News","content":"It also launches two major initiatives – the Global Implementation Accelerator and the Belém Mission to 1.5°C – to help countries deliver on their nationally determined contributions (NDCs), or national climate action plans, and adaptation plans.
The road to consensus at the latest Conference of the Parties to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), as the annual COPs are formally known, was anything but smooth.
UN climate chief Simon Stiell pointed to a series of major gains as COP30 closed in Belém: new strategies to accelerate Paris Agreement implementation, a push to triple adaptation finance, and commitments toward a just energy transition.
Tropical Forests Forever Fund: Raised $5.5 billion and now includes 53 participating countries; at least 20 per cent of resources go directly to Indigenous Peoples and local communities.
UNEZA Alliance: Public utility companies pledged $66 billion annually for renewable energy and $82 billion for transmission and storage.
Countries also agreed to develop a just transition mechanism, enhancing cooperation, technical support and capacity-building."}]}
